element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns dodge mechanics and weapon timing before entering fortress
Resource collection during low-stakes approach before core challenges
Introduces ammunition scarcity constraint before maze navigation

element_introduction_order.element: Flying enemies and space station debris
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Vertical and horizontal dodge patterns in scrolling shooter context
Transitional shift from arcade shooter to adventure-puzzle gameplay
Combat encounters within confined maze spaces
Primary goal revealed after initial fortress exploration
Time pressure and spatial memory test—exit location never matches entry point
Reviewers noted difficulty spike significantly after first fortress

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully reaching Air Lock (end of approach sequence)
Collecting energy and ammo pickups during Air Base
Defeating enemy clusters in fortress interior
typical_tension_triggers: Single-hit death state in lightship (Japanese version forces full restart; US version respawns at destruction point)
Dense enemy patterns in scrolling shooter approach
Post-core destruction escape timer with unknown exit location
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Two-phase structure: high-tempo arcade shooting contrasts with exploratory maze tension; escape sequence escalates tension through time pressure and spatial uncertainty

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate learning curve in first Air Base approach; introductory fortress interior navigation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Difficulty increases noticeably after first fortress completion
difficulty_curve.late_game: Progressively more challenging Air Fortresses with denser enemy patterns and more complex maze layouts
notable_spikes: Transition from Air Base shooter to fortress interior exploration (paradigm shift)
Post-core destruction escape sequence (time pressure + spatial amnesia)
Second fortress and beyond (Computer Entertainer and reviewers noted sharp difficulty increase)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each Air Fortress divided into Air Base (scrolling shooter approach with 3 lives/attempts) and Fortress interior (action-adventure maze with combat and exploration). Player collects ammunition and energy during approach to prepare for interior challenges.

Exit/escape point from fortress interior is never located at the entry point. After destroying core, player must navigate unfamiliar fortress layout under time pressure while explosion progresses. Tests spatial memory and forces exploration of previously unexplored areas.

Limited crash beam bullets available during Air Base phase. Player must choose between using powerful ammo early vs. conserving for fortress interior combat. Creates strategic resource management layer.

sources_used: Wikipedia - Air Fortress (gameplay sections: Air Base, Air Fortress, Reception)
TCRF - Air Fortress (regional differences and development notes)
